PlatformChannels

Channels & API Keys

Connect your AI agent to messaging platforms — Telegram, WhatsApp, and Discord — so it can chat with users in real time. Also add API keys for any external service your agents need to call.

Telegram

Recommended

The fastest way to deploy your AI agent. Create a Telegram bot, add the token, and your agent is live in minutes.

Setup Guide
  1. Open Telegram and search for @BotFather — the official bot for creating bots.
  2. Send /newbot and follow the prompts. Give your bot a display name (e.g., "Acme AI Assistant") and a username ending in bot (e.g., acme_ai_bot).
  3. BotFather will give you a token like: 123456789:ABCdefGHIjklMNOpqrsTUVwxyz.
    Copy and save this token — you will need it in the next step.
  1. Run the OpenClaw CLI command with your token:
    openclaw channels add telegram --token 123456789:ABCdefGHIjklMNOpqrsTUVwxyz
  2. Or add it via the dashboard: go to Settings → Channels → Telegram and paste the bot token into the token field.
  3. OpenClaw will validate the token automatically. A green Connected indicator confirms success.
Telegram bots never go offline — your AI agent is always on, responding instantly.
Use /start to reset the conversation and get a fresh context window.
You can set a bot username via @BotFather with the /setname command.
Telegram bots support deep links for easy user onboarding.
Never share your bot token publicly. Treat it like a password.
Telegram bots cannot initiate conversations — users must message them first.

WhatsApp

Business

Connect WhatsApp Business so users can message your AI agent directly from their contact list.

Setup Guide
  1. Option A — WhatsApp Business API (recommended for businesses)
    Official, scalable, supports rich media and templates.
  2. Option B — WhatsApp Web via Camoufox (unofficial, faster setup)
    Uses session cookies. Simpler to set up but less reliable for high volume.
  3. For most ClawOps users: start with Option A (Business API) for reliability and compliance, especially if you handle customer conversations at scale.
  1. Sign up at business.whatsapp.com and create a WhatsApp Business account.
  2. In the Meta Developer Console, create a new app and select WhatsApp as the product.
  3. Get your Phone Number ID (from the WhatsApp Business API setup page) and your Permanent Access Token (from Meta Business Suite).
  4. Add to OpenClaw:
    openclaw channels add whatsapp --token ACCESS_TOKEN --phone-id PHONE_ID
WhatsApp Business API is the official, reliable option — recommended for production.
The phone number connected must be a dedicated WhatsApp Business number.
Both individuals and businesses can use WhatsApp Business API.
WhatsApp Business API requires Meta Business verification — setup takes 1-3 business days.
WhatsApp has strict message templates. Non-template messages can only be sent within 24 hours of a user reply.

Discord

Community

Add your AI agent as a Discord bot to respond to slash commands, answer questions in channels, and moderate conversations.

Setup Guide
  1. Go to discord.com/developers/applications and click New Application. Give it a name (e.g., "ClawOps AI Bot").
  2. In the left sidebar, go to Bot and click Add Bot.
  3. Under the Token section, click Reset Token and copy the bot token. Store it securely — it will only be shown once.
  1. In the Discord Developer Portal, go to OAuth2 → URL Generator.
  2. Select scopes: bot and applications.commands.
  3. Select permissions: at minimum Send Messages and Read Message History.
  4. Copy the generated OAuth2 URL and open it in a new tab. Select your server and authorize the bot.
Discord bots can respond to slash commands (/) — very natural UX for community users.
Set up channel permissions so the bot only reads messages in designated channels.
Use Discord webhooks to post AI responses to channels automatically.
Discord bots must be invited to servers with the correct OAuth2 scopes — missing permissions will cause silent failures.
Discord has rate limits on message creation (10-50 per second). Design your agent with this in mind.

API Key Setup

Connect any external API to extend your AI agent's capabilities. Paste API keys in the dashboard and your agent can call those services directly.

Step-by-Step Guide
  1. Go to the provider's developer portal and sign in. Each provider below has its own process for generating API keys.
  2. Look for API Keys, Credentials, or OAuth in the settings or sidebar.
  3. Generate a new key. Some providers let you set expiry dates, IP allowlists, or scope restrictions — set these according to your needs.
  1. Go to Settings → API Keys in the ClawOps dashboard.
  2. Select the provider from the list (or choose "Custom API" for any REST API).
  3. Paste your API key and give it a label (e.g., "Production - OpenAI", "Test - DeepSeek").
  4. Click Save. The key is encrypted and stored securely — never logged or exposed to logs.
Supported Providers
Twitter / X
developer.twitter.com
Post, reply, and analyze tweets
LinkedIn
developer.linkedin.com
Company posts and messaging
Google AI Studio
aistudio.google.com
Gemini models & embeddings
OpenAI
platform.openai.com
GPT models, TTS, Whisper
ElevenLabs
elevenlabs.io
Voice synthesis & cloning
DeepSeek
platform.deepseek.com
DeepSeek models via OpenAI compat
Security: API keys are stored encrypted and never included in logs. Rotate keys regularly. If a key is compromised, delete it immediately from the dashboard and generate a new one from the provider.
FAQ
Can I connect multiple channels at the same time?
Yes. Telegram, WhatsApp, and Discord can all be connected simultaneously. Your AI agent will respond on all active channels independently.
Which channel is best for my business?
Telegram is the easiest to set up and most reliable. Discord is ideal for community-focused products. WhatsApp is best for B2C businesses with existing customer WhatsApp numbers.
How do I disconnect a channel?
Go to Settings → Channels → select the channel → click Disconnect. This immediately stops all incoming messages from that platform.
Are messages stored?
Message history is stored temporarily for context during your session. Consult the privacy policy for long-term storage details.
Can I use webhooks instead of persistent connections?
Yes. Both Discord and Telegram support webhook-based bot modes. This can reduce resource usage for lower-volume bots.
